assays.assay_desc: DRUGMATRIX: Protein Tyrosine Kinase, ligand-1 Receptor enzyme inhibition (substrate: Poly(Glu:Tyr))
DRUGMATRIX: Protein Tyrosine Kinase, TGTA (TGTA) enzyme inhibition (substrate: Poly(Glu:Tyr))
Inhibition of binding of Batrachotoxinin [3H]BTX-B to high affinity sites on voltage dependent sodium channels in a vesicular preparation from guinea pig cerebral cortex
Inhibitory activity against recombinant Trypanosoma cruzi (Trypanosoma cruzi) Trypanothione reductase
Inhibitory activity against recombinant Trypanosoma cruzi (Trypanosoma cruzi) Trypanothione reductase (linear competitive type)
The compound was tested for adenylate cyclase activity in rat corpus striatum
Binding affinity for human 5-hydroxytryptamine 6 receptor
Displacement of [3H]mibolerone from rat androgen receptor
Effective dose against Trypanosoma brucei) trypomastigotes in mouse peritoneal macrophages
Effective dose against Leishmania donovani (Leishmania donovani) amastigotes in mouse peritoneal macrophages
Effective dose against Trypanosoma cruzi (Trypanosoma cruzi) trypomastigotes in mouse peritoneal macrophages
Anti MDR activity was expressed as MDR ratio or MDR fold reversal in doxorubicin resistant human breast carcinoma tumor cell line MCF-7/DOX
Inhibition of compound against MES-SA/DX5 cell line was determined using P-glycoprotein assay
Antagonist activity at human 5HT3A receptor expressed in HEK293 cells assessed as inhibition of serotonin-induced inward Na+ current at >= 10 uM
Antifungal activity against yeast AD1-8u expressing Candida albicans CaCdr1p by agar disk diffusion assay
Antifungal activity against yeast AD1-8u expressing Candida albicans CaMdr1p by agar disk diffusion assay
Oral bioavailability in human
Volume of distribution at steady state in human at 2.5 mg, iv administered as bolus dose

Moderate liver toxicity, defined via clinical-chemistry results: ALT or AST serum activity 6 times the normal upper limit (N) or alkaline phosphatase serum activity of 1.7 N. Value is number of references indexed. [column 'BIOL' in source]
Highest frequency of moderate liver toxicity observed during clinical trials, expressed as a percentage. [column '% BIOL' in source]

Acute liver toxicity defined via clinical observations and clear clinical-chemistry results: serum ALT or AST activity > 6 N or serum alkaline phosphatases activity > 1.7 N. This category includes cytolytic, choleostatic and mixed liver toxicity. Value is number of references indexed. [column 'AIGUE' in source]
Highest frequency of acute liver toxicity observed during clinical trials, expressed as a percentage. [column '% AIGUE' in source]

Cytolytic liver toxicity, either proven histopathologically or where the ratio of maximal ALT or AST activity above normal to that of Alkaline Phosphatase is > 5 (see ACUTE). Value is number of references indexed. [column 'CYTOL' in source]

Choleostatic liver toxicity, either proven histopathologically or where the ratio of maximal ALT or AST activity above normal to that of Alkaline Phosphatase is < 2 (see ACUTE). Value is number of references indexed. [column 'CHOLE' in source]
Severe hepatitis, defined as possibly life-threatening liver failure or through clinical observations. Value is number of references indexed. [column 'MASS' in source]

Chronic liver disease either proven histopathologically, or through a chonic elevation of serum amino-transferase activity after 6 months. Value is number of references indexed. [column 'CHRON' in source]
